sections.paragraph:
ἀντί [Skt. ánti ‘over against’, Lat. ante ‘before’; prim. in local sense ‘over against, in front of’] prep. w. gen.

—1. as marker of one entity replaced by or exchanged for another, instead of, in place of: of succession Mt 2:22 (one king after another); J 1:16 (grace after grace); of an entity supplanted by another Lk 11:11; 1 Cor 11:15; of one procedure replacing another ἀντὶ τοῦ λέγειν ὑμᾶς instead of your saying Js 4:15; of price offered in exchange Hb 12:16; in imagery Mt 20:28; Mk 10:45.

—2. of requital or off set, for Mt 5:38; Ro 12:17; 1 Th 5:15; 1 Pt 3:9. ἀνϑ’ ὧν because Lk 1:20; 2 Th 2:10; therefore Lk 12:3; Eph 5:31.

—3. of a generous gesture, in behalf of, for Mt 17:27.
